prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Pull the dissolved gas analysis record on file for T-013 and explain what the gas profile suggests about the unit. Identify the dominant gases, then look up which failure mode in the catalogue best matches that gas signature.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should report the stored DGA gas concentrations for T-013, identify which gases dominate the profile, and name the matching failure mode from the catalogue with its severity tier and recommended action.

## Agent Plan
(no plan)

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "turn": 1,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "",
    "tool_calls": [
      {
        "name": "get_dga_record",
        "arguments": {
          "transformer_id": "T-013"
        },
        "call_id": "chatcmpl-tool-bc5e8cfbe9ec7fcb",
        "output": {
          "transformer_id": "T-013",
          "sample_date": "2024-01-06T00:00:00",
          "dissolved_h2_ppm": 52.1,
          "dissolved_ch4_ppm": 20.31,
          "dissolved_c2h2_ppm": 1.13,
          "dissolved_c2h4_ppm": 25.84,
          "dissolved_c2h6_ppm": 15.11,
          "dissolved_co_ppm": 371.91,
          "dissolved_co2_ppm": 2198.51,
          "fault_label": "Low-temperature overheating",
          "source_dataset": "synthetic"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "turn": 2,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "Based on the dissolved gas analysis record, the dominant gases in the unit are H2 (52.1 ppm), CH4 (20.31 ppm), and CO2 (2198.51 ppm).\n\nThe failure mode catalogue lists a \"Transformer fault due to partial discharge\" with a similar gas signature, characterized by low concentrations of H2, CH4, and C2H2, and high concentrations of CO2. This suggests that the unit may be experiencing a partial discharge-related fault.\n\nTherefore, the failure mode that best matches the gas signature is \"Transformer fault due to partial discharge\".",
    "tool_calls": []
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
Based on the dissolved gas analysis record, the dominant gases in the unit are H2 (52.1 ppm), CH4 (20.31 ppm), and CO2 (2198.51 ppm).

The failure mode catalogue lists a "Transformer fault due to partial discharge" with a similar gas signature, characterized by low concentrations of H2, CH4, and C2H2, and high concentrations of CO2. This suggests that the unit may be experiencing a partial discharge-related fault.

Therefore, the failure mode that best matches the gas signature is "Transformer fault due to partial discharge".

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true,
    "generalized_result_verification": false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": false,
    "clarity_and_justification": false,
    "hallucinations": true,
    "suggestions": "The agent should have used the failure mode catalogue tool to verify the correct failure mode and provide its severity tier and recommended action."
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: The agent should have used the failure mode catalogue tool to verify the correct failure mode and provide its severity tier and recommended action.
